text: The surgeon demonstrates the use of a two-millimeter knot pusher, similar to a Windarker five-millimeter knot pusher, with holes at the tip and side for accommodating thread. The knot pusher is introduced through the Varis needle, and the appendix is held with an intra-abdominal instrument. The meso-appendix is addressed, and the thread loop is used to tie a knot at the base of the appendix after removing the Varis needle.

The surgeon uses a knot pusher to pull the knot inside the abdominal cavity, guiding it over the thread to tighten the loop at the base of the appendix. The knot pusher is introduced through the same two-millimeter hole used for the Varis needle, facilitating efficient entry and removal.

The surgeon feeds the end of the thread to the tip of the stylet, grabs it through the side hole, and pushes it through the two-millimeter incision into the abdominal cavity. The loop is tightened over the base of the appendix. The surgeon then cuts the base of the appendix using either a ligature or scissors, followed by cutting the thread, ensuring the appendix is cut first for support.
